NEXT SUNDAY’S TIES

On Sunday next important ties in the
Leinster championships will be played at
New Ross and Tullamore. At the former
venue, Wexford and Kilkenny meet in
Senior and Junior football and in Junior hur
ling, This is a programme of no ordinary
attractiveness, and should ensure big crowd
at the historic Wexford town. At Tulla-
more, Leix are contesting the Junior burl.
ing and football championships with Offaly,
and these neighbouring counties are sare
to provide keen competition. In Munster
the big fixture isthe Thomond Feis final
between Clare and Tipperary, which, in all
likelihood, will indicate the destiny of
Munster hurling honours this season. “The
‘Thomond Feis competition is, perbaps, the
most important tournament held outside
the championship ares, and, as in the past,
ie will probably be productive of a monster
gathering.
